_meta.description: Trilingual dictionary: English, Garo, and Hindi with classifier engine and examples

classifier_engine._description: Garo uses noun classifiers (like counters) that must be attached when referring to quantities of a noun. Use the correct classifier based on the noun category.

you (subject) [new].example: Na·a bano re·ang·a? = Where did you go?

ong·ja.example: Nama ong·ja = it is not good

dak·ja.example: Indake dak·ja = don't do like that

have.example: Anga kolom donga = I have a pen

have/has.example: Anga kolom donga = I have a pen

don't have.example: A’gimiko dongja = I don't have time

have you eaten?.example: Mi cha·jok ma? = Have you eaten?

have/had done something.example: Anga dakaha = I have done/made it

have you eaten rice?.garo: Na·a mi cha·aha ma? / Mi cha·jok ma?

_examples.english: I want to eat a mango

_examples.hindi: अदरक स्वास्थ्य के लिए अच्छा है

_examples.english: Two crows are on the tree
Three hens are in the yard
There are ants on the food
I saw a frog near the river
_examples.hindi: मैंने नदी के पास एक मेंढक देखा

ka·sa·na.example: Anga nang·na ka·sa·a = I love you

dak·na.english: to work / to do / to make

agan·na.english: to speak / to say / to tell

ni·na.english: to look / to see / to watch

on·a.example: Ang-na chi on·bo = Give me water

_examples.english: The forest is full of trees
The book is on the bed

pring.example: Pring-ni baji chet = 8 o'clock in the morning

_examples.hindi: उसके पास सोने की अंगूठी है

_examples.english: I am going to the market

anti.example: Anti-chi re·na = to go to the market

a·ba.example: A·ba o·na = to clear/open a forest for cultivation

_examples.english: The market is to the left
The school is to the north

_examples.hindi: मेरा घाव ठीक हो रहा है

mi·cha·na.example: Anga mi cha·a = I ate rice

bre·na.example: Anga mi bre·a = I bought rice

_examples.hindi: मैं गाड़ी से जा रहा हूँ

gari.example: Gari-ko sal·na = to drive a vehicle

_examples.hindi: गाँव का मुखिया बोल रहा है

jrim·a.example: Ia jrim·a ong·a = This is heavy

jrim·bea.example: Ia jrim·bea = This is very heavy

dong·a.example: Donga = it is there / I have

_examples.hindi: वह गरीब है लेकिन खुश है

_examples.english: I came because I love you
_examples.hindi: मैं आया क्योंकि मैं तुमसे प्यार करता हूँ

_examples.english: If you go, I will come
_examples.hindi: अगर तुम जाओ, तो मैं आऊँगा

ba·na.english: to carry (usually a child on the back)
ba·na.example: Bi·sako ba·na = to carry the child on the back

bil·na.example: Do·o bil·enga = the bird is flying

gong·grak·na.english: to be quick / to be active

verb_conjugations._description: Garo verb conjugation patterns across tenses and moods

grammar_patterns._description: Common Garo grammatical structures and sentence patterns

subject_object_verb_order.example: Anga mi cha·a = I rice ate = I ate rice

question_formation.pattern: Add '?' or use question word (bano=where, maia=what, sawa=who)
question_formation.english: Form questions with particles or question words
examples: Na·a bano re·angenga? = Where are you going?

conversational_phrases._description: Common phrases organized by conversation context

garo_phrases.english: It is good / All is well
How do I get to the market?
What do you want to buy?
What is there in the market?

idiomatic_expressions._description: Garo idioms, cultural phrases, and figurative expressions

figurative_language.example: Anga gisik jrim·a ong·a = I am feeling very sad
Ua gisik nima ong·a = He is very happy
Anga ok gisik dak·a = I thought about it
Kami bol tang·a = We worked very hard
Anga meng·sa dak·a = I did it alone

cultural_expressions.example: Hai dong rakka = Making a sacred vow
Anga Garo-sa jong·gipa = I am a Garo person
A·chik ku·sik porai·na = Learning Garo language
Nongkrem dong dak·a = Performing the sacred dance

proverbs_and_sayings.example: Daba·chu nang·sa-gong ka·sa·ani dak·gipa ong·a = A patient and loving person will succeed

proverbs_and_sayings.meaning: Basic needs must be met first
proverbs_and_sayings.example: Mi sa·dik-chi gisik sa·dik ong·a = When you're hungry, your mind troubles

sentence_structures._description: Common sentence patterns in Garo with examples
